adds.rationale: Pure-play small-launch rocket company, now publicly traded since 2021-08-25 after the Vector SPAC merger closed at a $4.8B equity valuation. Cleaner exposure to the rockets/spacecraft next-wave thesis than ARKX, whose holdings are diluted with defense primes and aerospace incumbents. Profile explicitly names rockets as a next-wave bet.

Cheniere Energy is the largest US LNG exporter and the cleanest US-listed beneficiary of Europe's natural gas crisis. TTF gas hit a record 79 EUR/MWh in mid-September 2021 with EU storage at multi-decade lows (74.6%) heading into winter, structurally tightening seaborne LNG demand. Fits the profile's geopolitical-energy non-tech wave bucket.

removes.rationale: Diluted space-thesis vehicle: top holdings are split across defense primes, aerospace incumbents, and tangential infrastructure rather than pure-play launch/spacecraft names. Replacing with RKLB gives a cleaner read on the rockets next-wave bet without changing the slot's wave bucket.

Defense prime whose primary driver is the US defense budget, not the profile's named next-waves. The Aerojet Rocketdyne acquisition (announced Dec 2020, stockholder-approved Mar 2021) is still pending regulatory clearance with no closing date, so the rockets-adjacent upside is uncertain. Freeing the slot for a cleaner geopolitical-energy add (LNG) tightens wave alignment.

summary: Two-for-two swap at cap: tighten the rockets slot by replacing the diluted ARKX ETF with pure-play RKLB (newly public 2021-08-25), and reallocate the LMT defense-prime slot to LNG to capture the dated European gas crunch as a geopolitical-energy non-tech wave bet. Keeps AAPL/MSFT/GOOGL/NVDA AI core and ISRG (robotics) + URA (nuclear/uranium, with Sprott-driven spot surge dated Sep 2021) intact.
